violations.finding: Direct python3 invocation without venv activation: `python3 scripts/graphify-enrich.py --task-id <task_id> --context-file <context_file_path>`
violations.recommendation: Use: `source venv/bin/activate && python scripts/graphify-enrich.py --task-id <task_id> --context-file <context_file_path>` (or `source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python ~/.claude/scripts/graphify-enrich.py` depending on project-relative vs global path context)

violations.finding: Direct python3 invocation in bash code block without venv activation: `python3 "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/graphify-query.py" \ --task-id "$DEV_SESSION_ID" \ --requirement-file "$REQUIREMENT_DOC"`
violations.recommendation: Add venv activation before the python3 call: `source venv/bin/activate && python "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/graphify-query.py" --task-id "$DEV_SESSION_ID" --requirement-file "$REQUIREMENT_DOC"`. Consistent with the existing pattern at commands/dev.md:802 which uses `source venv/bin/activate && python3 scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py`.

violations.finding: Direct python3 invocation in subagent dispatch prompt text without venv activation: `Run: python3 $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/graphify-enrich.py --task-id <DEV_SESSION_ID> --context-file <context_json_path>`
Direct python3 invocation without venv activation: `python3 "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/graphify-maintain.py" update`
violations.recommendation: Add venv activation: `source venv/bin/activate && python "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/graphify-maintain.py" update`

violations.finding: Decimal step numbering: 'Step 7.5' used to identify the agent's position in the /dev pipeline
violations.recommendation: Replace with integer-based language: e.g. 'You run between Step 7 and Step 8 of the /dev pipeline' or 'You run after BA-QA validation (Step 7) and before DEV dispatch (Step 8)'. Avoid decimal steps entirely.

violations.finding: Decimal step references introduced in the Graphify consumption block: 'graphify subagent at Step 7.5' (line 116) and 'Step 1.5 Bash call' and 'Step 7.5' (line 121)
violations.recommendation: Replace all decimal step references: 'graphify subagent (between Step 7 and Step 8)' and 'the pre-BA Bash hydrator (between Step 1 and Step 2)' and 'graphify subagent (between Step 7 and Step 8)'.

violations.finding: Decimal step reference introduced in Graphify fail gate G2 text: 'graphify ran successfully at Step 1.5 and status=ok or status=degraded'
violations.recommendation: Replace with: 'graphify ran successfully (pre-BA Bash hydrator, between Step 1 and Step 2) and status=ok or status=degraded'.

violations.finding: Chinese characters newly introduced in the BA Reference Resolution section: lines 640 and 646 contain '之前, 已有, 现有, 原来的' as implicit reference trigger words that BA must detect. These are not mascot rank labels and are not in a docs/dev/ticket-*.md file.
violations.recommendation: Remove the inline Chinese characters from the agent file. Two compliant alternatives: (1) Use English-only labels ('Chinese equivalents: see docs/dev/ticket-20260527-132200.md') with a pointer to the ticket; (2) Use Unicode escapes in a code-block context (e.g. \u4e4b\u524d) so they are not literal CJK text. Preserve the Chinese keywords in docs/dev/ticket-20260527-132200.md as the verbatim-binding citation source.

violations.finding: Chinese characters newly introduced in Fail Gate G2: '之前/已有/现有/原来的' inline in the gate condition. Not a mascot rank label; not in a docs/dev/ticket-*.md file.
violations.recommendation: Use English-only text: 'implicit reference trigger words (Chinese equivalents documented in docs/dev/ticket-20260527-132200.md)' with the Chinese forms preserved in that ticket file only.

violations.finding: Chinese characters in the IMPLICIT_REFERENCE_WORDS list: '之前', '已有', '现有', '原来的' appear as literal string values in a Python script (new file). The comment '# Chinese' identifies them explicitly. Scripts must be English-only.
violations.recommendation: Replace literal CJK strings with Unicode escapes: '\u4e4b\u524d', '\u5df2\u6709', '\u73b0\u6709', '\u539f\u6765\u7684'. These are functionally identical for string matching but do not contain literal CJK code points, satisfying the English-only rule while preserving runtime behavior.

notes.standard_1_no_inline_code: Not violated. The python3 code blocks in agents/graphify.md and commands/dev.md are single-script invocations (no bash loops, conditional logic, or file-manipulation inline code). They delegate all logic to external scripts per Standard 1 intent.
notes.standard_2_no_hardcoded_values: Not violated. graphify_lib.py line 98 has Path('/var/tmp/claude-graphify') as default but is parameterized via CLAUDE_GRAPHIFY_CACHE_ROOT env override. All other paths derive from CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR or are passed as CLI args.
notes.standard_5_naming: Not violated. No enhance-*, fast-*, optimize-*, *-v2, improved-*, better-*, new-* patterns in changed files.
notes.standard_6_cjk_preexisting: agents/ba.md and agents/qa.md contained NO CJK in the base commit (eb3bfd2). The CJK was confirmed introduced in this cycle's commits. agents/style-inspector.md CJK (mascot rank labels) is pre-existing and exempt per spec-20260518-225715 §5.1.
notes.standard_7_merge_similar: Not violated. The four graphify scripts (graphify_lib.py, graphify-query.py, graphify-enrich.py, graphify-maintain.py) have distinct, non-overlapping purposes: shared library, Step 1.5 pre-BA query, Step 7.5 enrichment, and cache lifecycle management respectively.
notes.standard_8_concise_docs: Not violated for new files. agents/graphify.md (83 lines) is concise and functional. The commands/dev.md verbose sections (Agent Development Use Cases, Example E2E Workflow) are pre-existing, not introduced in this diff.
notes.standard_10_dangling_refs: Not violated. All three referenced scripts (graphify-query.py, graphify-enrich.py, graphify-maintain.py) exist on disk. graphify_lib.py shared library exists.
notes.standard_11_merge_conflicts: No merge conflict markers (<<<<<<, =======, >>>>>>) found in any of the changed files. The detect-merge-conflicts.sh script reported conflicts in non-changed files (INDEX.md descriptions containing === lines in content, pre-existing elsewhere) which are outside the --changed-files scope.
notes.codex_feedback_item2: Codex flagged INDEX.md:4861,4863, scripts/INDEX.md:66-68, scripts/README.md:45-47 for 'Step 1.5'/'Step 7.5' text in generated index entries. These are auto-generated index files outside the style-inspector scope (.claude/commands/*.md and .claude/agents/*.md). The finding is noted but classified out_of_scope for style standards 1-11 as currently defined.
notes.codex_feedback_item3: Codex flagged Orchestrator Prompt Purity (CLAUDE.md Production Catastrophe Lesson 11 / 'HOW vs WHAT' rule) for commands/dev.md:689-697 Graphify dispatch prompt. This is a CLAUDE.md global constraint, not a style standard 1-11. Out of style-inspector scope.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed no false positives in all 10 draft violations. Codex flagged two additional potential issues: (1) 'Step 1.5'/'Step 7.5' text appearing in auto-generated INDEX/README files — classified out_of_scope for style-inspector (not in .claude/commands/*.md or .claude/agents/*.md); (2) Orchestrator Prompt Purity violation in commands/dev.md:689-697 — classified out_of_scope for style standards 1-11 (this is a CLAUDE.md global constraint). Codex also suggested using Unicode escapes for CJK in scripts/graphify-query.py as a compliant alternative (logged as OBSERVATION_ONLY, recommendation updated accordingly).

findings.issue: OBSERVATION_ONLY: No false positives in draft violations #1-#10. All are introduced in scope and severity critical is correct per style-inspector.md which says all violations are critical.
MISSED_CRITICAL (Codex): 'Step 1.5'/'Step 7.5' text appears in auto-generated index files INDEX.md:4861,4863, scripts/INDEX.md:66-68, scripts/README.md:45-47 (derived from script docstrings).
findings.proposed_fix: Replace script docstring descriptions referencing Step 1.5/Step 7.5 with non-decimal wording; regenerate indexes. e.g. 'graphify-enrich.py — pre-DEV focused subgraph extractor' and 'graphify-query.py — deterministic pre-BA graph hydrator (between Step 1 and Step 2)'
findings.rationale: Style-inspector Standard 4 scope is .md files in .claude/ (commands/*.md, agents/*.md). Auto-generated INDEX/README files are not in this scope. Finding noted as advisory — the script docstrings should be updated when the decimal step naming is fixed in the agent/command files.

findings.issue: MISSED_CRITICAL (Codex): commands/dev.md:689-697 Graphify dispatch prompt uses HOW/tool/shell syntax (FIRST ACTION: Read..., Run: python3...) conflicting with Orchestrator Prompt Purity (CLAUDE.md Lesson 11).
findings.proposed_fix: Replace dispatch prompt with WHAT-only + DO NOT: 'Produce Graphify enrichment artifacts for <DEV_SESSION_ID> and patch <context_json_path> with graph_context; treat unavailable/skipped states as advisory. DO NOT analyze requirements, make implementation decisions, modify production code, or block DEV on Graphify failure.'
findings.rationale: This is a CLAUDE.md Production Catastrophe Lesson 11 constraint (not a style standard 1-11). Out of style-inspector scope. Flagged to close.md as advisory for the orchestrator-level review.

findings.issue: OBSERVATION_ONLY: Recommendations for English-only violations #8-#10 should preserve runtime behavior of CJK trigger-word detection.
findings.proposed_fix: Use Unicode escapes in scripts/graphify-query.py (e.g. '\u4e4b\u524d' for '之前') to preserve runtime string matching without literal CJK codepoints. In agent .md files, reference the Chinese equivalents via a ticket pointer.
findings.rationale: Good practical suggestion incorporated into the recommendation for violation #10 (graphify-query.py). Unicode escapes are functionally equivalent for string matching and satisfy the English-only rule.

findings.issue: OBSERVATION_ONLY: Do not expand the draft with pre-existing violations from agents/qa.md Step 10.1-10.5 or older bare python3 references.
findings.proposed_fix: Mark pre-existing findings introduced_in_diff: false per --changed-files mode scoping.
findings.rationale: Already handled correctly. All 10 violations are tagged introduced_in_diff: true and are confirmed new in this cycle's diff.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Codex confirmed all 10 violations. Two Codex MISSED_CRITICAL findings are classified out_of_scope for style standards 1-11 (INDEX files and Orchestrator Prompt Purity). Unicode-escape recommendation for CJK violations incorporated into violation #10 recommendation.
